pattern: The skip-one constraint means each house's best outcome depends only on the two previous decisions, reducing the recurrence to two rolling variables and a single linear scan.
hints: For each house you face the same binary choice: take its value and skip the previous house, or skip it and keep whatever you had through the previous house — which of those two is larger?

You never need to look back further than two steps: let prev2 be the best up to two houses ago and prev1 be the best up to one house ago, then the best including the current house is max(prev2 + current, prev1).
Initialize prev2 = 0 and prev1 = 0, then for each value in nums set new = max(prev2 + val, prev1), shift prev2 = prev1, prev1 = new; the answer is prev1 after the loop.

edge_inputs.description: statement example 2: best subset skips adjacent
maximum length with all maximum values
strictly increasing values tempt greedy to always take the last
alternating spike pattern where every other house has a large value

stress.description: random arrays of varying length up to 100 with values in [0, 400]
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 400) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
